# Packages

A short stack does most of the work in this book. `ggplot2` is the foundation;
everything else either builds figures on top of it or supplies the modeling
output we plot.

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(ggplot2)
library(hvtiPlotR)        # house plots, themes, and PPT/PDF export
library(hvtiRutilities)   # data dictionaries, labels, manifests
library(gt)               # publication tables
library(patchwork)        # compose multi-panel figures
```

What each one is for:

- **hvtiPlotR** -- the core of the book. It wraps the figures we publish into a
  two-step API: an `hv_*()` constructor validates the data and returns an object,
  and `plot()` turns that object into a bare ggplot. It also carries the house
  themes (`theme_hv_manuscript()`, `theme_hv_poster()`, the PowerPoint themes)
  and the save helpers.
- **hvtiRutilities** -- the data-governance side: data dictionaries, variable
  labels, dataset manifests, and the synthetic-data generators we use so the
  examples run without touching real patient data.
- **ggRandomForests**, **randomForestSRC**, **varPro** -- the random-forest
  stack. `randomForestSRC` fits the forest, `varPro` does variable selection, and
  `ggRandomForests` turns either one into a ggplot. These load in the random
  forest chapters where they are used.
- **TemporalHazard** -- our R port of the SAS/C HAZARD module, for parametric and
  nonparametric hazard modeling. It loads in its own chapter.
- **gt** -- publication-quality tables. We use it directly for now; a companion
  `hvtiRtables` package is planned to give tables the same house treatment the
  plots get.
- **patchwork** -- composes several plots into one figure with shared labels and
  panel tags.

You do not need all of these for any single figure. Each chapter loads what it
uses in its first chunk.
